methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
Crusoe Energy is a Series E artificial intelligence and software company founded by Chase Lochmiller and Cully Cavness. The target's last post-money valuation was $10.0B, with $1.375B raised to date. The peer universe comprises five same-stage, same-sector private comparables (xAI, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, WEKA, Modal) alongside one public comparable (Palantir Technologies, ticker PLTR).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $10.0B base, yielding $14.0B. Peer-median methodology identified a median valuation of $3.8B across the five private comparables, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion: a floor of $1.1B (Modal) and a ceiling of $250.0B (xAI), reflecting wide variance in AI & Software stage-E outcomes.

The triangulation of these two methods implies a valuation range of $3.2B to $16.1B, with a median of $8.9B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and market dynamics within the sector. The peer-median method suggests Crusoe's last $10.0B valuation may sit above typical Stage E cohort medians, while stage-progression implies modest upside from that baseline. Key uncertainties include the comparability of xAI's $250.0B outlier valuation and the applicability of public-market comps to the private AI infrastructure landscape.
